From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Mexican baseball player (born 1995)

In this [Spanish name](/wiki/Spanish_name "Spanish name"), the first or paternal [surname](/wiki/Surname "Surname") is  *Ríos* and the second or maternal family name is  *Alfaro*.

Baseball player

| Francisco Ríos | |
| --- | --- |
| Ríos with the Lansing Lugnuts in 2016 | |
| Free agent | |
| **[Pitcher](/wiki/Pitcher "Pitcher")** | |
| **Born:**  (1995-05-06) May 6, 1995 (age 30) [Monclova](/wiki/Monclova "Monclova"), [Coahuila](/wiki/Coahuila "Coahuila"), [Mexico](/wiki/Mexico "Mexico") | |
| **Bats:** Right  **Throws:** Right | |
| **[Stats](https://www.baseball-reference.com/register/player.fcgi?id=rios--002fra) at [Baseball Reference](/wiki/Baseball_Reference "Baseball Reference")** [Edit this at Wikidata](https://www.wikidata.org/wiki/Q62006166#P1826 "Edit this at Wikidata") | |
|  | |
| Medals  |  |  | | --- | --- | |  | | | Men's [baseball](/wiki/Baseball "Baseball") | | | | Representing  [Mexico](/wiki/Mexico "Mexico") | | | | [2019 WBSC Premier12](/wiki/2019_WBSC_Premier12 "2019 WBSC Premier12") | | | | Bronze medal – third place | [2019 Tokyo](/wiki/2019_WBSC_Premier12 "2019 WBSC Premier12") | [National team](/wiki/Mexico_national_baseball_team "Mexico national baseball team") | | |

**Jesus Francisco Ríos Alfaro** (born May 6, 1995) is a Mexican professional [baseball](/wiki/Baseball "Baseball") [pitcher](/wiki/Pitcher "Pitcher") who is a free agent.

## Career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Francisco_R%C3%ADos&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Career")]

### Toronto Blue Jays

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Francisco_R%C3%ADos&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Toronto Blue Jays")]

Ríos signed with the [Toronto Blue Jays](/wiki/Toronto_Blue_Jays "Toronto Blue Jays") as an international free agent on July 20, 2012.[[1]](#cite_note-1) He made his professional debut in 2013 with the [Dominican Summer League Blue Jays](/wiki/Dominican_Summer_League_Blue_Jays "Dominican Summer League Blue Jays"), appearing in 15 games. In 521⁄3 innings pitched, Ríos would post a 4–6 [win–loss record](/wiki/Win%E2%80%93loss_record_(pitching) "Win–loss record (pitching)"), 4.47 [earned run average](/wiki/Earned_run_average "Earned run average") (ERA), and 48 strikeouts.[[2]](#cite_note-RíosBRM-2) In the offseason, he played with the [Tomateros de Culiacán](/wiki/Tomateros_de_Culiac%C3%A1n "Tomateros de Culiacán") of the [Mexican Pacific League](/wiki/Mexican_Pacific_League "Mexican Pacific League").[[2]](#cite_note-RíosBRM-2) Ríos spent 2014 with the Rookie-Advanced [Bluefield Blue Jays](/wiki/Bluefield_Blue_Jays "Bluefield Blue Jays"), and pitched to a 3–2 record, 5.91 ERA, and 38 strikeouts in 531⁄3 innings.[[2]](#cite_note-RíosBRM-2)

Ríos was promoted to the Low–A [Vancouver Canadians](/wiki/Vancouver_Canadians "Vancouver Canadians") for the 2015 season, and in 15 total appearances, posted a 3–6 win–loss record, 4.27 ERA, and 59 strikeouts in 651⁄3 innings pitched.[[2]](#cite_note-RíosBRM-2) He was assigned to the Single–A [Lansing Lugnuts](/wiki/Lansing_Lugnuts "Lansing Lugnuts") to open the 2016 season. In 6 starts, Ríos went 2–0 with a 1.20 ERA and 43 strikeouts in 30 total innings.[[2]](#cite_note-RíosBRM-2)[[3]](#cite_note-3) He was promoted to the High–A [Dunedin Blue Jays](/wiki/Dunedin_Blue_Jays "Dunedin Blue Jays") on May 10.[[4]](#cite_note-4) In June he was selected to play in the [All-Star Futures Game](/wiki/All-Star_Futures_Game "All-Star Futures Game").[[5]](#cite_note-5) Ríos made 21 starts and four relief appearances in 2016, and pitched to a 7–6 record, 2.91 ERA, and 108 strikeouts in 1202⁄3 innings.[[2]](#cite_note-RíosBRM-2) Ríos played the entire 2017 season for the [Double-A](/wiki/Double-A_(baseball) "Double-A (baseball)") [New Hampshire Fisher Cats](/wiki/New_Hampshire_Fisher_Cats "New Hampshire Fisher Cats"), pitching to a 3–9 record, 4.29 ERA, and 63 strikeouts in 86 total innings.[[2]](#cite_note-RíosBRM-2)

On May 28, 2019, Ríos was loaned to the [Diablos Rojos del México](/wiki/Diablos_Rojos_del_M%C3%A9xico "Diablos Rojos del México") of the [Mexican League](/wiki/Mexican_Baseball_League "Mexican Baseball League"). In 19 games, he compiled a 2–1 record and 3.94 ERA with 24 strikeouts over 32 innings pitched.[[6]](#cite_note-MiLBStats-6) In 9 games split between New Hampshire and the Triple–A [Buffalo Bisons](/wiki/Buffalo_Bisons "Buffalo Bisons"), Ríos accumulated a 6.19 ERA with 10 strikeouts across 16 innings. Ríos elected free agency following the season on November 4.[[7]](#cite_note-7)

### New York Mets

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Francisco_R%C3%ADos&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: New York Mets")]

On December 13, 2019, Ríos signed a minor league contract with the [New York Mets](/wiki/New_York_Mets "New York Mets") organization.[[8]](#cite_note-8) He did not play in a game in 2020 due to the cancellation of the minor league season because of the [COVID-19 pandemic](/wiki/COVID-19_pandemic "COVID-19 pandemic").[[9]](#cite_note-9) Ríos became a free agent on November 2, 2020.[[10]](#cite_note-10)

### Acereros de Monclova

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Francisco_R%C3%ADos&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Acereros de Monclova")]

On May 20, 2021, Ríos signed with the [Acereros de Monclova](/wiki/Acereros_de_Monclova "Acereros de Monclova") of the [Mexican League](/wiki/Mexican_Baseball_League "Mexican Baseball League"). In 13 starts, he went 4–4 with a 4.39 ERA and 64 strikeouts over 67+2⁄3 innings. Ríos returned in 2022, but was limited to only 6 appearances due to injuries, posting an inflated 17.05 ERA over 6.1 innings. In 2023, he registered a 1–4 record with a 9.25 ERA and 17 strikeouts over 24+1⁄3 innings pitched.

### Tigres de Quintana Roo

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Francisco_R%C3%ADos&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Tigres de Quintana Roo")]

On June 27, 2023, Ríos was loaned to the [Tigres de Quintana Roo](/wiki/Tigres_de_Quintana_Roo "Tigres de Quintana Roo") of the [Mexican League](/wiki/Mexican_Baseball_League "Mexican Baseball League") for the remainder of the 2023 season.[[6]](#cite_note-MiLBStats-6) In 3 starts for Quintana Roo, he posted an 0–2 record and 4.66 ERA with 6 strikeouts across 9+2⁄3 innings pitched. Ríos was placed on the reserve list on July 19.

On May 11, 2024, Ríos was returned to the [Acereros de Monclova](/wiki/Acereros_de_Monclova "Acereros de Monclova") for the 2024 season. He did not appear with the team and spent the whole season on the reserve list. Ríos was released by Monclova on December 23.[[11]](#cite_note-11)

### Saraperos de Saltillo

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Francisco_R%C3%ADos&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Saraperos de Saltillo")]

On December 26, 2024, Ríos signed with the [Saraperos de Saltillo](/wiki/Saraperos_de_Saltillo "Saraperos de Saltillo") of the [Mexican League](/wiki/Mexican_Baseball_League "Mexican Baseball League").[[12]](#cite_note-12) He was released by Saltillo on April 8, 2025.[[13]](#cite_note-13)
